functions.author: Kael'Vorr
functions.title: Anatomy of a Brute
pages:
Anatomy of a Brute

A practical guide to the infernal siege creature called the Hell Brute.

Origin

Hell Brutes guarded fortresses in a realm where walls grew back. Their answer was to become stronger than regeneration.

Selection

Kael'Vorr chose them because they solve problems without changing methods. He considers this limitation scientifically useful.

Body

Dense flesh surrounds a furnace-hot core. Demon steel fragments grow through the body like a second skeleton.

Temperament

A Brute is not mindless. It simply considers subtlety an inefficient distance between itself and a target.

Combat

Keep space, use terrain, and never assume one stagger has ended its charge. A Brute's momentum is part of the weapon.

Demon Ash

Brutes shed more ash than Emberstalkers. Their constant internal heat consumes tissue even while they fight.

Demon Hearts

Their hearts are rare, durable catalysts used throughout demon crafting and Chaos Altar recipes.

Corrupted XP

The Brute can drop Corrupted XP Orbs. A powered Demon Altar can also make them from sacrifices.

Demon Skull

Craft with Demon Ash, Bone Blocks, and a Demon Heart. Three skulls are required in the Demon Lord ritual.

Pentagram

Craft with Demon Ash, Redstone, and a Demon Heart. Two pentagrams complete the ritual frame.

Lord Ritual

Build a cross with a central Pentagram, another below, and Skulls above and on both sides.

Chaos Craft

Demon Steel remembers impact. Kael'Vorr uses that memory in weapons that become more dangerous through use.

Kael'Vorr's Note

Elegant? No. Repeatable? Extremely.
